name_policy: Personal names remain in original English.
spoiler_policy: Only information available through each current paragraph is used.

blocks.original_text: It’s the Hand’s tourney that’s the cause of all the trouble, my lords,” the Commander of the City Watch complained to the king’s council.
blocks.explanation_zh: City Watch commander向council抱怨Hand’s tourney造成所有麻烦。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Hand’s tourney再次显示命名如何转移责任：Robert命令，Eddard承受治安和声誉后果。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在tourney治理、Jon与Stannis调查轨迹、证人可靠性、Gendry外貌或Eddard血缘推断。

blocks.original_text: “The king’s tourney,” Ned corrected, wincing. “I assure you, the Hand wants no part of it.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard纠正这是king’s tourney，并明确自己不想参与。
blocks.literary_note_zh: tourney人口冲击令city violence升级，Robert仍缺席；Eddard用guards和预算处理后果，却更渴望北方家庭。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在tourney治理、Jon与Stannis调查轨迹、证人可靠性、Gendry外貌或Eddard血缘推断。

blocks.original_text: “Call it what you will, my lord. Knights have been arriving from all over the realm, and for every knight we get two freeriders, three craftsmen, six men-at-arms, a dozen merchants, two dozen whores, and more thieves than I dare guess. This cursed heat had half the city in a fever to start, and now with all these visitors . . . last night we had a drowning, a tavern riot, three knife fights, a rape, two fires, robberies beyond count, and a drunken horse race down the Street of the Sisters. The night before a woman’s head was found in the Great Sept, floating in the rainbow pool. No one seems to know how it got there or who it belongs to.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Janos列骑士带来的大量随从、商贩、妓女和盗贼，以及一夜内暴力、火灾、抢劫与无头女尸。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 数字递增从knight扩散到economy与crime，tourney不是孤立竞技，而是人口冲击城市系统。
blocks.background_zh: **城市治理：** tourney吸引参赛者及大量随行经济人口，叠加酷热造成City Watch超负荷。

blocks.original_text: “How dreadful,” Varys said with a shudder.
blocks.explanation_zh: Varys颤抖说可怕。
blocks.literary_note_zh: tourney人口冲击令city violence升级，Robert仍缺席；Eddard用guards和预算处理后果，却更渴望北方家庭。

blocks.original_text: Lord Renly Baratheon was less sympathetic. “If you cannot keep the king’s peace, Janos, perhaps the City Watch should be commanded by someone who can.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Renly说若Janos维持不了king’s peace就应换command。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Renly把结构性过载归给commander能力，Janos则把责任推回资源不足。

blocks.original_text: Stout, jowly Janos Slynt puffed himself up like an angry frog, his bald pate reddening. “Aegon the Dragon himself could not keep the peace, Lord Renly. I need more men.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Janos像怒蛙般膨起，称Aegon也无法维持并要求更多men。
blocks.literary_note_zh: tourney人口冲击令city violence升级，Robert仍缺席；Eddard用guards和预算处理后果，却更渴望北方家庭。

blocks.original_text: “How many?” Ned asked, leaning forward. As ever, Robert had not troubled himself to attend the council session, so it fell to his Hand to speak for him.
blocks.explanation_zh: Robert照例缺席，Eddard以Hand身份问需要多少。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Robert空席使Hand speak for him；Eddard既否认tourney所有权又必须处理其全部后果。

blocks.original_text: “As many as can be gotten, Lord Hand.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Janos要能得到的全部。
blocks.literary_note_zh: tourney人口冲击令city violence升级，Robert仍缺席；Eddard用guards和预算处理后果，却更渴望北方家庭。

blocks.original_text: “Hire fifty new men,” Ned told him. “Lord Baelish will see that you get the coin.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard批准新增五十人并让Littlefinger筹钱。
blocks.literary_note_zh: tourney人口冲击令city violence升级，Robert仍缺席；Eddard用guards和预算处理后果，却更渴望北方家庭。

blocks.original_text: “I will?” Littlefinger said.
blocks.explanation_zh: Littlefinger惊问自己负责。
blocks.literary_note_zh: tourney人口冲击令city violence升级，Robert仍缺席；Eddard用guards和预算处理后果，却更渴望北方家庭。

blocks.original_text: “You will. You found forty thousand golden dragons for a champion’s purse, surely you can scrape together a few coppers to keep the king’s peace.” Ned turned back to Janos Slynt. “I will also give you twenty good swords from my own household guard, to serve with the Watch until the crowds have left.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard以四万champion purse反讽财政优先，再借二十名household guards维持治安。
blocks.literary_note_zh: champion purse与king’s peace资金并置，Eddard用预算反讽迫使Littlefinger显出价值排序。
blocks.background_zh: **兵力：** Eddard新增五十gold cloaks并临时借二十名Stark guards；这些guards由Alyn指挥。

blocks.original_text: “All thanks, Lord Hand,” Slynt said, bowing. “I promise you, they shall be put to good use.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Janos鞠躬承诺善用。
blocks.literary_note_zh: tourney人口冲击令city violence升级，Robert仍缺席；Eddard用guards和预算处理后果，却更渴望北方家庭。

blocks.original_text: When the Commander had taken his leave, Eddard Stark turned to the rest of the council. “The sooner this folly is done with, the better I shall like it.” As if the expense and trouble were not irksome enough, all and sundry insisted on salting Ned’s wound by calling it “the Hand’s tourney,” as if he were the cause of it. And Robert honestly seemed to think he should feel honored!
blocks.explanation_zh: Janos离开后Eddard盼tourney尽快结束，并恼怒全城把Robert命令归名于自己。
blocks.literary_note_zh: tourney人口冲击令city violence升级，Robert仍缺席；Eddard用guards和预算处理后果，却更渴望北方家庭。

blocks.original_text: “The realm prospers from such events, my lord,” Grand Maester Pycelle said. “They bring the great the chance of glory, and the lowly a respite from their woes.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Pycelle说盛会使great获glory、lowly暂忘痛苦。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Pycelle给出bread-and-circuses式解释：glory与respite都可是真实收益，却不抹去暴力成本。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在tourney治理、Jon与Stannis调查轨迹、证人可靠性、Gendry外貌或Eddard血缘推断。

blocks.original_text: “And put coins in many a pocket,” Littlefinger added. “Every inn in the city is full, and the whores are walking bowlegged and jingling with each step.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Littlefinger补充商业收益，并用妓女收入作粗俗例子。
blocks.literary_note_zh: tourney人口冲击令city violence升级，Robert仍缺席；Eddard用guards和预算处理后果，却更渴望北方家庭。

blocks.original_text: Lord Renly laughed. “We’re fortunate my brother Stannis is not with us. Remember the time he proposed to outlaw brothels? The king asked him if perhaps he’d like to outlaw eating, shitting, and breathing while he was at it. If truth be told, I ofttimes wonder how Stannis ever got that ugly daughter of his. He goes to his marriage bed like a man marching to a battlefield, with a grim look in his eyes and a determination to do his duty.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Renly讲Stannis曾想禁brothel，继而嘲笑其婚姻和女儿外貌。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 笑话攻击Stannis性格、妻女与性能力，council娱乐建立在缺席者无法回应上。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在tourney治理、Jon与Stannis调查轨迹、证人可靠性、Gendry外貌或Eddard血缘推断。

blocks.original_text: Ned had not joined the laughter. “I wonder about your brother Stannis as well. I wonder when he intends to end his visit to Dragonstone and resume his seat on this council.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard不笑，反问Stannis何时从Dragonstone返council。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard拒笑并把Stannis拉回office duty，说明他关注缺席的政治含义而非私人怪异。
blocks.background_zh: **council席位：** Stannis是Robert弟弟和small council member，king北行后移居Dragonstone未说明返期。

blocks.original_text: “No doubt as soon as we’ve scourged all those whores into the sea,” Littlefinger replied, provoking more laughter.
blocks.explanation_zh: Littlefinger用清除妓女笑话回避，引发更多笑声。
blocks.literary_note_zh: tourney人口冲击令city violence升级，Robert仍缺席；Eddard用guards和预算处理后果，却更渴望北方家庭。

blocks.original_text: “I have heard quite enough about whores for one day,” Ned said, rising. “Until the morrow.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard厌烦终止会议。
blocks.literary_note_zh: tourney人口冲击令city violence升级，Robert仍缺席；Eddard用guards和预算处理后果，却更渴望北方家庭。

blocks.original_text: Harwin had the door when Ned returned to the Tower of the Hand. “Summon Jory to my chambers and tell your father to saddle my horse,” Ned told him, too brusquely.
blocks.explanation_zh: 回Tower后Eddard命Harwin叫Jory并让Hullen备马，语气过于急硬。
blocks.literary_note_zh: tourney人口冲击令city violence升级，Robert仍缺席；Eddard用guards和预算处理后果，却更渴望北方家庭。

blocks.original_text: “As you say, my lord.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Harwin服从。
blocks.literary_note_zh: tourney人口冲击令city violence升级，Robert仍缺席；Eddard用guards和预算处理后果，却更渴望北方家庭。

blocks.original_text: The Red Keep and the “Hand’s tourney” were chafing him raw, Ned reflected as he climbed. He yearned for the comfort of Catelyn’s arms, for the sounds of Robb and Jon crossing swords in the practice yard, for the cool days and cold nights of the north.
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard觉得Red Keep与tourney磨破自己，渴望Catelyn、孩子练剑声和北方冷空气。
blocks.literary_note_zh: chafing raw把制度厌恶写成皮肤磨伤，与Daenerys骑行伤痛的身体语言遥相呼应。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在tourney治理、Jon与Stannis调查轨迹、证人可靠性、Gendry外貌或Eddard血缘推断。

blocks.original_text: In his chambers he stripped off his council silks and sat for a moment with the book while he waited for Jory to arrive. The Lineages and Histories of the Great Houses of the Seven Kingdoms, With Descriptions of Many High Lords and Noble Ladies and Their Children, by Grand Maester Malleon. Pycelle had spoken truly; it made for ponderous reading. Yet Jon Arryn had asked for it, and Ned felt certain he had reasons. There was something here, some truth buried in these brittle yellow pages, if only he could see it. But what? The tome was over a century old. Scarcely a man now alive had yet been born when Malleon had compiled his dusty lists of weddings, births, and deaths.
blocks.explanation_zh: 他换下council silks等待Jory，翻Malleon世系巨册，确信百年前婚生死名单中藏有Jon所见truth。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 百年旧书不可能直接记录当前children，Jon必在寻找跨代pattern；Eddard尚未知道pattern是什么。
blocks.background_zh: **书目校勘：** 页面图像确认标题为Descriptions；PDF文字层误作Descliptions，精读已修正。

blocks.original_text: He opened to the section on House Lannister once more, and turned the pages slowly, hoping against hope that something would leap out at him. The Lannisters were an old family, tracing their descent back to Lann the Clever, a trickster from the Age of Heroes who was no doubt as legendary as Bran the Builder, though far more beloved of singers and taletellers. In the songs, Lann was the fellow who winkled the Casterlys out of Casterly Rock with no weapon but his wits, and stole gold from the sun to brighten his curly hair. Ned wished he were here now, to winkle the truth out of this damnable book.
blocks.explanation_zh: 他反复读House Lannister，想借Lann the Clever的wits从书中套出答案。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Lann传奇靠wit夺城，Eddard此刻也需要非武力方式从静态名单中winkle truth。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在tourney治理、Jon与Stannis调查轨迹、证人可靠性、Gendry外貌或Eddard血缘推断。

blocks.original_text: A sharp rap on the door heralded Jory Cassel. Ned closed Malleon’s tome and bid him enter. “I’ve promised the City Watch twenty of my guard until the tourney is done,” he told him. “I rely on you to make the choice. Give Alyn the command, and make certain the men understand that they are needed to stop fights, not start them.” Rising, Ned opened a cedar chest and removed a light linen undertunic. “Did you find the stableboy?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Jory到后，Eddard让他挑二十guards由Alyn领队协助City Watch，并问stablehand。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 二十guards既帮助治安，也把Eddard有限五十人进一步分散，正常行政有安全代价。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在tourney治理、Jon与Stannis调查轨迹、证人可靠性、Gendry外貌或Eddard血缘推断。

blocks.original_text: “The watchman, my lord,” Jory said. “He vows he’ll never touch another horse.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Jory纠正此人现为watchman且发誓不再碰马。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 世系书暂不显答案；Jory汇总四名servants，Stannis与Jon共同骑行、brothel和armorer三条线索反复出现。

blocks.original_text: “What did he have to say?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard问其证词。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 世系书暂不显答案；Jory汇总四名servants，Stannis与Jon共同骑行、brothel和armorer三条线索反复出现。

blocks.original_text: “He claims he knew Lord Arryn well. Fast friends, they were.” Jory snorted. “The Hand always gave the lads a copper on their name days, he says. Had a way with horses. Never rode his mounts too hard, and brought them carrots and apples, so they were always pleased to see him.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Jory讽刺他所谓与Jon好友，只记得Jon善待马匹、给lads name-day copper。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 世系书暂不显答案；Jory汇总四名servants，Stannis与Jon共同骑行、brothel和armorer三条线索反复出现。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在tourney治理、Jon与Stannis调查轨迹、证人可靠性、Gendry外貌或Eddard血缘推断。

blocks.original_text: “Carrots and apples,” Ned repeated. It sounded as if this boy would be even less use than the others. And he was the last of the four Littlefinger had turned up. Jory had spoken to each of them in turn. Ser Hugh had been brusque and uninformative, and arrogant as only a new-made knight can be. If the Hand wished to talk to him, he should be pleased to receive him, but he would not be questioned by a mere captain of guards . . . even if said captain was ten years older and a hundred times the swordsman. The serving girl had at least been pleasant. She said Lord Jon had been reading more than was good for him, that he was troubled and melancholy over his young son’s frailty, and gruff with his lady wife. The potboy, now cordwainer, had never exchanged so much as a word with Lord Jon, but he was full of oddments of kitchen gossip: the lord had been quarreling with the king, the lord only picked at his food, the lord was sending his boy to be fostered on Dragonstone, the lord had taken a great interest in the breeding of hunting hounds, the lord had visited a master armorer to commission a new suit of plate, wrought all in pale silver with a blue jasper falcon and a mother-of-pearl moon on the breast. The king’s own brother had gone with him to help choose the design, the potboy said. No, not Lord Renly, the other one, Lord Stannis.
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard回顾四名证人：Ser Hugh拒绝Jory询问；girl说Jon忧儿病且夫妻紧张；potboy提供争吵、Dragonstone foster、猎犬breeding和与Stannis访armorer等gossip。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 四人证词价值不按阶级排列：拒答squire未必优于gossip potboy，关键线索可能来自边缘观察者。
blocks.background_zh: **证人汇总：** Ser Hugh、kitchen girl、former potboy、former stablehand各提供不同层级信息；均为Jory转述。

blocks.original_text: “Did our watchman recall anything else of note?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard问watchman还有什么重要记忆。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 世系书暂不显答案；Jory汇总四名servants，Stannis与Jon共同骑行、brothel和armorer三条线索反复出现。

blocks.original_text: “The lad swears Lord Jon was as strong as a man half his age. Often went riding with Lord Stannis, he says.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Jory说Jon身体强健，常与Stannis骑行。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Jon被描述为strong直接反驳Pycelle以年老frailty解释急病的便利程度，但不排除sudden illness。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在tourney治理、Jon与Stannis调查轨迹、证人可靠性、Gendry外貌或Eddard血缘推断。

blocks.original_text: Stannis again, Ned thought. He found that curious. Jon Arryn and he had been cordial, but never friendly. And while Robert had been riding north to Winterfell, Stannis had removed himself to Dragonstone, the Targaryen island fastness he had conquered in his brother’s name. He had given no word as to when he might return. “Where did they go on these rides?” Ned asked.
blocks.explanation_zh: Stannis再次出现令Eddard警觉；他在Robert北行后去Dragonstone且无返期。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Stannis与Jon反复同行且突然离城形成可调查pattern，仍不能单凭相关性判罪。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在tourney治理、Jon与Stannis调查轨迹、证人可靠性、Gendry外貌或Eddard血缘推断。

blocks.original_text: “The boy says that they visited a brothel.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Jory说二人骑去brothel。
blocks.literary_note_zh: brothel与两人已知性格不符，因此更可能是调查地点而非娱乐；这是推断，尚未确认。

blocks.original_text: “A brothel?” Ned said. “The Lord of the Eyrie and Hand of the King visited a brothel with Stannis Baratheon?” He shook his head, incredulous, wondering what Lord Renly would make of this tidbit. Robert’s lusts were the subject of ribald drinking songs throughout the realm, but Stannis was a different sort of man; a bare year younger than the king, yet utterly unlike him, stern, humorless, unforgiving, grim in his sense of duty.
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard不信严厉守责的Stannis会与Jon逛brothel，并想到Renly会如何取笑。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 世系书暂不显答案；Jory汇总四名servants，Stannis与Jon共同骑行、brothel和armorer三条线索反复出现。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在tourney治理、Jon与Stannis调查轨迹、证人可靠性、Gendry外貌或Eddard血缘推断。

blocks.original_text: “The boy insists it’s true. The Hand took three guardsmen with him, and the boy says they were joking of it when he took their horses afterward.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Jory说stablehand坚持真实，三名guards回来取马时还拿此事开玩笑。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 世系书暂不显答案；Jory汇总四名servants，Stannis与Jon共同骑行、brothel和armorer三条线索反复出现。

blocks.original_text: “A pity Lysa carried them off to the Vale,” Ned said dryly. “The gods are doing their best to vex us. Lady Lysa, Maester Colemon, Lord Stannis . . . everyone who might actually know the truth of what happened to Jon Arryn is a thousand leagues away.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard讽刺Lysa把guards、Colemon带远，加上Stannis，知情者全不在城。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 世系书暂不显答案；Jory汇总四名servants，Stannis与Jon共同骑行、brothel和armorer三条线索反复出现。
blocks.background_zh: **缺席关键人：** Lysa带走guards与Colemon，Stannis在Dragonstone，使最接近Jon调查者难以询问。

blocks.original_text: “Will you summon Lord Stannis back from Dragonstone?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Jory问是否召Stannis回来。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 世系书暂不显答案；Jory汇总四名servants，Stannis与Jon共同骑行、brothel和armorer三条线索反复出现。

blocks.original_text: “Not yet,” Ned said. “Not until I have a better notion of what this is all about and where he stands.” The matter nagged at him. Why did Stannis leave? Had he played some part in Jon Arryn’s murder? Or was he afraid? Ned found it hard to imagine what could frighten Stannis Baratheon, who had once held Storm’s End through a year of siege, surviving on rats and boot leather while the Lords Tyrell and Redwyne sat outside with their hosts, banqueting in sight of his walls.
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard暂缓，因不知Stannis立场；他推测可能涉案或害怕，又回想其坚守Storm’s End的勇气。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard同时提出guilt与fear两假设，没有只选择符合既有印象的一种。
blocks.background_zh: **历史：** Stannis曾在Robert’s Rebellion中守Storm’s End近一年，经历饥饿仍未投降，故Eddard认为他不易被吓走。

blocks.original_text: “Bring me my doublet, if you would. The grey, with the direwolf sigil. I want this armorer to know who I am. It might make him more forthcoming.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard要穿灰色direwolf doublet，让armorer认清Hand身份以更愿开口。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard以Hand身份公开前往Street of Steel，在Mott推销中突然追问，发现Jon和Stannis真正想看的不是armor而是boy。

blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard以Hand身份公开前往Street of Steel，在Mott推销中突然追问，发现Jon和Stannis真正想看的不是armor而是boy。

blocks.original_text: “Yet it seems that he was not invited on these rides.” Ned was not sure what to make of Renly, with all his friendly ways and easy smiles. A few days past, he had taken Ned aside to show him an exquisite rose gold locklet. Inside was a miniature painted in the vivid Myrish style, of a lovely young girl with doe’s eyes and a cascade of soft brown hair. Renly had seemed anxious to know if the girl reminded him of anyone, and when Ned had no answer but a shrug, he had seemed disappointed. The maid was Loras Tyrell’s sister Margaery, he’d confessed, but there were those who said she looked like Lyanna. “No,” Ned had told him, bemused. Could it be that Lord Renly, who looked so like a young Robert, had conceived a passion for a girl he fancied to be a young Lyanna? That struck him as more than passing queer.
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard注意Renly未同行，又回忆Renly展示Margaery miniature、试探她是否像Lyanna。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Renly问Margaery是否像Lyanna，目的不明；Eddard关于passion的解释是当下个人猜测。
blocks.background_zh: **人物：** Margaery Tyrell是Loras sister；Renly声称有人说她像Lyanna，但Eddard明确回答不像。

blocks.original_text: Jory held out the doublet, and Ned slid his hands through the armholes. “Perhaps Lord Stannis will return for Robert’s tourney,” he said as Jory laced the garment up the back.
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard猜Stannis或许会为tourney回来。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard以Hand身份公开前往Street of Steel，在Mott推销中突然追问，发现Jon和Stannis真正想看的不是armor而是boy。

blocks.original_text: “That would be a stroke of fortune, my lord,” Jory said.

blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard以Hand身份公开前往Street of Steel，在Mott推销中突然追问，发现Jon和Stannis真正想看的不是armor而是boy。

blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard以Hand身份公开前往Street of Steel，在Mott推销中突然追问，发现Jon和Stannis真正想看的不是armor而是boy。

blocks.original_text: Ned nodded. “The gods help this potboy if he’s sent me off haring after shadows.” It was a slim enough staff to lean on, but the Jon Arryn that Ned Stark had known was not one to wear jeweled and silvered plate. Steel was steel; it was meant for protection, not ornament. He might have changed his views, to be sure. He would scarcely have been the first man who came to look on things differently after a few years at court . . . but the change was marked enough to make Ned wonder.
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard担心追逐shadows，但Jon不爱装饰甲，突然订银蓝falcon armor足够反常。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 反常commission才使armor gossip值得追；优秀调查依赖了解Jon平常价值偏好。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在tourney治理、Jon与Stannis调查轨迹、证人可靠性、Gendry外貌或Eddard血缘推断。

blocks.original_text: “Is there any other service I might perform?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Jory问还有何服务。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard以Hand身份公开前往Street of Steel，在Mott推销中突然追问，发现Jon和Stannis真正想看的不是armor而是boy。

blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard以Hand身份公开前往Street of Steel，在Mott推销中突然追问，发现Jon和Stannis真正想看的不是armor而是boy。

blocks.original_text: Ned’s favorite horse was saddled and waiting in the yard. Varly and Jacks fell in beside him as he rode through the yard. Their steel caps and shirts of mail must have been sweltering, yet they said no word of complaint. As Lord Eddard passed beneath the King’s Gate into the stink of the city, his grey and white cloak streaming from his shoulders, he saw eyes everywhere and kicked his mount into a trot. His guard followed.
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard带Varly、Jacks骑出King’s Gate，看见处处eyes便加速。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard以Hand身份公开前往Street of Steel，在Mott推销中突然追问，发现Jon和Stannis真正想看的不是armor而是boy。

blocks.original_text: He looked behind him frequently as they made their way through the crowded city streets. Tomard and Desmond had left the castle early this morning to take up positions on the route they must take, and watch for anyone following them, but even so, Ned was uncertain. The shadow of the King’s Spider and his little birds had him fretting like a maiden on her wedding night.
blocks.explanation_zh: 虽已让Tomard、Desmond沿路监视跟踪者，Varys网络仍使他焦虑。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 安排counter-surveillance后仍焦虑说明不知道未知watchers数量时，安全措施无法带来确定。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在tourney治理、Jon与Stannis调查轨迹、证人可靠性、Gendry外貌或Eddard血缘推断。

blocks.original_text: The Street of Steel began at the market square beside the River Gate, as it was named on maps, or the Mud Gate, as it was commonly called. A mummer on stilts was striding through the throngs like some great insect, with a horde of barefoot children trailing behind him, hooting. Elsewhere, two ragged boys no older than Bran were dueling with sticks, to the loud encouragement of some and the furious curses of others. An old woman ended the contest by leaning out of her window and emptying a bucket of slops on the heads of the combatants. In the shadow of the wall, farmers stood beside their wagons, bellowing out, “Apples, the best apples, cheap at twice the price,” and “Blood melons, sweet as honey,” and “Turnips, onions, roots, here you go here, here you go, turnips, onions, roots, here you go here.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Street of Steel始于Mud Gate市场；高跷演员、stick fight、泼污水老妇和叫卖农民构成拥挤街景。
blocks.literary_note_zh: stick-dueling boys与tourney knights形成社会缩影；竞技文化从elite渗入street play。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在tourney治理、Jon与Stannis调查轨迹、证人可靠性、Gendry外貌或Eddard血缘推断。

blocks.original_text: The Mud Gate was open, and a squad of City Watchmen stood under the portcullis in their golden cloaks, leaning on spears. When a column of riders appeared from the west, the guardsmen sprang into action, shouting commands and moving the carts and foot traffic aside to let the knight enter with his escort. The first rider through the gate carried a long black banner. The silk rippled in the wind like a living thing; across the fabric was blazoned a night sky slashed with purple lightning. “Make way for Lord Beric!” the rider shouted. “Make way for Lord Beric!” And close behind came the young lord himself, a dashing figure on a black courser, with red-gold hair and a black satin cloak dusted with stars. “Here to fight in the Hand’s tourney, my lord?” a guardsman called out to him. “Here to win the Hand’s tourney,” Lord Beric shouted back as the crowd cheered.
blocks.explanation_zh: City Watch为Beric Dondarrion队伍清路；purple lightning旗帜入城，他宣称来赢Hand’s tourney。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Beric把Hand’s tourney称呼继续公开化，证明Eddard已无法靠纠正council改变社会名称。
blocks.background_zh: **人物：** Beric Dondarrion为紫闪电黑夜sigil的年轻lord，来参加tourney；本段只建立登场。

blocks.original_text: Ned turned off the square where the Street of Steel began and followed its winding path up a long hill, past blacksmiths working at open forges, freeriders haggling over mail shirts, and grizzled ironmongers selling old blades and razors from their wagons. The farther they climbed, the larger the buildings grew. The man they wanted was all the way at the top of the hill, in a huge house of timber and plaster whose upper stories loomed over the narrow street. The double doors showed a hunting scene carved in ebony and weirwood. A pair of stone knights stood sentry at the entrance, armored in fanciful suits of polished red steel that transformed them into griffin and unicorn. Ned left his horse with Jacks and shouldered his way inside.
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard沿逐渐富裕扩大的smith shops上坡，到有ebony/weirwood门与红钢stone knights的豪华armorer house。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 越上坡建筑越大，把craft hierarchy和wealth转化为城市地形。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在tourney治理、Jon与Stannis调查轨迹、证人可靠性、Gendry外貌或Eddard血缘推断。

blocks.original_text: The slim young serving girl took quick note of Ned’s badge and the sigil on his doublet, and the master came hurrying out, all smiles and bows. “Wine for the King’s Hand,” he told the girl, gesturing Ned to a couch. “I am Tobho Mott, my lord, please, please, put yourself at ease.” He wore a black velvet coat with hammers embroidered on the sleeves in silver thread, Around his neck was a heavy silver chain and a sapphire as large as a pigeon’s egg. “If you are in need of new arms for the Hand’s tourney, you have come to the right shop.” Ned did not bother to correct him. “My work is costly, and I make no apologies for that, my lord,” he said as he filled two matching silver goblets. “You will not find craftsmanship equal to mine anywhere in the Seven Kingdoms, I promise you. Visit every forge in King’s Landing if you like, and compare for yourself. Any village smith can hammer out a shirt of mail; my work is art.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Tobho Mott见Hand badge热情接待，自夸工作是昂贵art而非普通mail。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Mott先读badge与sigil再切换服务姿态，Eddard穿正式doublet的策略立刻生效。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在tourney治理、Jon与Stannis调查轨迹、证人可靠性、Gendry外貌或Eddard血缘推断。

blocks.original_text: Ned sipped his wine and let the man go on. The Knight of Flowers bought all his armor here, Tobho boasted, and many high lords, the ones who knew fine steel, and even Lord Renly, the king’s own brother. Perhaps the Hand had seen Lord Renly’s new armor, the green plate with the golden antlers? No other armorer in the city could get that deep a green; he knew the secret of putting color in the steel itself, paint and enamel were the crutches of a journeyman. Or mayhaps the Hand wanted a blade? Tobho had learned to work Valyrian steel at the forges of Qohor as a boy. Only a man who knew the spells could take old weapons and forge them anew. “The direwolf is the sigil of House Stark, is it not? I could fashion a direwolf helm so real that children will run from you in the street,” he vowed.
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard让他继续炫耀Loras、Renly绿甲和Qohor重铸Valyrian steel本领。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Mott自我推销看似冗长，却建立其材料、客户和Valyrian technique专业权威。
blocks.background_zh: **工艺：** Tobho Mott来自Qohor，声称掌握重铸Valyrian steel所需技术；他是王都顶级armorer。

blocks.original_text: Ned smiled. “Did you make a falcon helm for Lord Arryn?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard突然问是否为Jon做falcon helm。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard借对方推销间隙突然问falcon helm，减少Mott预先准备答案时间。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在tourney治理、Jon与Stannis调查轨迹、证人可靠性、Gendry外貌或Eddard血缘推断。

blocks.original_text: Tobho Mott paused a long moment and set aside his wine. “The Hand did call upon me, with Lord Stannis, the king’s brother. I regret to say, they did not honor me with their patronage.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Mott停顿放酒，承认Jon与Stannis来访却没购买armor。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 长pause和set aside wine是可见谨慎，不等同有罪；他最终纠正potboy误解：没有commission。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在tourney治理、Jon与Stannis调查轨迹、证人可靠性、Gendry外貌或Eddard血缘推断。

blocks.original_text: Ned looked at the man evenly, saying nothing, waiting. He had found over the years that silence sometimes yielded more than questions. And so it was this time.
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard保持沉默等待，经验告诉他silence比questions产出更多。
blocks.literary_note_zh: silence创造对方填补空白的压力，是Eddard少数适应宫廷间接方法的调查技巧。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在tourney治理、Jon与Stannis调查轨迹、证人可靠性、Gendry外貌或Eddard血缘推断。

blocks.original_text: “They asked to see the boy,” the armorer said, “so I took them back to the forge.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Mott补充二人要求看the boy，他带他们去forge。
blocks.literary_note_zh: the boy取代armor成为Jon/Stannis真实访问目的，旧线索被重新解释为cover或误传。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在tourney治理、Jon与Stannis调查轨迹、证人可靠性、Gendry外貌或Eddard血缘推断。

blocks.original_text: “The boy,” Ned echoed. He had no notion who the boy might be. “I should like to see the boy as well.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard不知道boy是谁，却也要求见。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard以Hand身份公开前往Street of Steel，在Mott推销中突然追问，发现Jon和Stannis真正想看的不是armor而是boy。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在tourney治理、Jon与Stannis调查轨迹、证人可靠性、Gendry外貌或Eddard血缘推断。

blocks.original_text: Tobho Mott gave him a cool, careful look. “As you wish, my lord,” he said with no trace of his former friendliness. He led Ned out a rear door and across a narrow yard, back to the cavernous stone barn where the work was done. When the armorer opened the door, the blast of hot air that came through made Ned feel as though he were walking into a dragon’s mouth. Inside, a forge blazed in each corner, and the air stank of smoke and sulfur. Journeymen armorers glanced up from their hammers and tongs just long enough to wipe the sweat from their brows, while bare-chested apprentice boys worked the bellows.
blocks.explanation_zh: Mott态度由亲切转谨慎，带他进入如dragon mouth般炽热烟硫forge。
blocks.literary_note_zh: dragon mouth比喻把forge塑成火腹，也为Gendry的力量和钢铁身份搭台。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在tourney治理、Jon与Stannis调查轨迹、证人可靠性、Gendry外貌或Eddard血缘推断。

blocks.original_text: The master called over a tall lad about Robb’s age, his arms and chest corded with muscle. “This is Lord Stark, the new Hand of the King,” he told him as the boy looked at Ned through sullen blue eyes and pushed back sweat-soaked hair with his fingers. Thick hair, shaggy and unkempt and black as ink. The shadow of a new beard darkened his jaw. “This is Gendry. Strong for his age, and he works hard. Show the Hand that helmet you made, lad.” Almost shyly, the boy led them to his bench, and a steel helm shaped like a bull’s head, with two great curving horns.
blocks.explanation_zh: Mott介绍与Robb同龄、黑发蓝眼强壮勤奋的Gendry，并展示其bull horn helm。
blocks.literary_note_zh: black hair、blue eyes、jaw和muscle都是可见特征；血缘意义要等Eddard比较与其他证据。
blocks.background_zh: **Gendry：** 约Robb年龄的armorer apprentice，黑发蓝眼、强壮勤奋；bull helm为自己制作。

blocks.original_text: Ned turned the helm over in his hands. It was raw steel, unpolished but expertly shaped. “This is fine work. I would be pleased if you would let me buy it.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard赞helm工艺并提出购买。
blocks.literary_note_zh: forge中Gendry以黑发蓝眼、力量、bull helm和固执登场；Jon曾专问其mother，Eddard由外貌形成血缘判断。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在tourney治理、Jon与Stannis调查轨迹、证人可靠性、Gendry外貌或Eddard血缘推断。

blocks.original_text: The boy snatched it out of his hands. “It’s not for sale.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Gendry直接夺回说不卖。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Gendry在Hand面前夺helm，固执既危险也显示作品对其自我身份不可替代。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在tourney治理、Jon与Stannis调查轨迹、证人可靠性、Gendry外貌或Eddard血缘推断。

blocks.original_text: Tobho Mott looked horror-struck. “Boy, this is the King’s Hand. If his lordship wants this helm, make him a gift of it. He honors you by asking.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Mott惊恐命他把helm赠Hand才是honor。
blocks.literary_note_zh: forge中Gendry以黑发蓝眼、力量、bull helm和固执登场；Jon曾专问其mother，Eddard由外貌形成血缘判断。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在tourney治理、Jon与Stannis调查轨迹、证人可靠性、Gendry外貌或Eddard血缘推断。

blocks.original_text: “I made it for me,” the boy said stubbornly.
blocks.explanation_zh: Gendry固执说为自己做。
blocks.literary_note_zh: forge中Gendry以黑发蓝眼、力量、bull helm和固执登场；Jon曾专问其mother，Eddard由外貌形成血缘判断。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在tourney治理、Jon与Stannis调查轨迹、证人可靠性、Gendry外貌或Eddard血缘推断。

blocks.original_text: “A hundred pardons, my lord,” his master said hurriedly to Ned. “The boy is crude as new steel, and like new steel would profit from some beating. That helm is journeyman’s work at best. Forgive him and I promise I will craft you a helm like none you have ever seen.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Mott道歉并威胁beat，称只能算journeyman work，愿另制更好helm。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Mott用beating恢复主从礼仪，却也立刻愿用更贵作品赔偿权贵，显示工坊生存压力。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在tourney治理、Jon与Stannis调查轨迹、证人可靠性、Gendry外貌或Eddard血缘推断。

blocks.original_text: “He’s done nothing that requires my forgiveness. Gendry, when Lord Arryn came to see you, what did you talk about?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard说无需原谅，转问Jon来访谈话。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard拒绝要求forgiveness，保护Gendry免因自己身份被罚，也迅速把对话拉回调查。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在tourney治理、Jon与Stannis调查轨迹、证人可靠性、Gendry外貌或Eddard血缘推断。

blocks.original_text: “He asked me questions is all, m’lord.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Gendry说只问了一些问题。
blocks.literary_note_zh: forge中Gendry以黑发蓝眼、力量、bull helm和固执登场；Jon曾专问其mother，Eddard由外貌形成血缘判断。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在tourney治理、Jon与Stannis调查轨迹、证人可靠性、Gendry外貌或Eddard血缘推断。

blocks.original_text: “What sort of questions?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard追问哪类。
blocks.literary_note_zh: forge中Gendry以黑发蓝眼、力量、bull helm和固执登场；Jon曾专问其mother，Eddard由外貌形成血缘判断。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在tourney治理、Jon与Stannis调查轨迹、证人可靠性、Gendry外貌或Eddard血缘推断。

blocks.original_text: The boy shrugged. “How was I, and was I well treated, and if I liked the work, and stuff about my mother. Who she was and what she looked like and all.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Gendry说Jon问生活待遇、工作喜好以及母亲身份外貌。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Jon问的不是铠甲而是mother外貌，说明他调查血缘而非工艺。
blocks.background_zh: **Jon调查：** Jon Arryn询问Gendry生活、待遇和mother身份外貌；Stannis同行但沉默。

blocks.original_text: “What did you tell him?” Ned asked.
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard问他如何回答。
blocks.literary_note_zh: forge中Gendry以黑发蓝眼、力量、bull helm和固执登场；Jon曾专问其mother，Eddard由外貌形成血缘判断。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在tourney治理、Jon与Stannis调查轨迹、证人可靠性、Gendry外貌或Eddard血缘推断。

blocks.original_text: The boy shoved a fresh fall of black hair off his forehead. “She died when I was little. She had yellow hair, and sometimes she used to sing to me, I remember. She worked in an alehouse.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Gendry说母亲幼时死去，黄发、会唱歌、在alehouse工作。
blocks.literary_note_zh: yellow-haired mother与Gendry black hair构成遗传观察材料，但单个家庭特征仍不足以科学证明父亲。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在tourney治理、Jon与Stannis调查轨迹、证人可靠性、Gendry外貌或Eddard血缘推断。

blocks.original_text: “Did Lord Stannis question you as well?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard问Stannis是否也提问。
blocks.literary_note_zh: forge中Gendry以黑发蓝眼、力量、bull helm和固执登场；Jon曾专问其mother，Eddard由外貌形成血缘判断。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在tourney治理、Jon与Stannis调查轨迹、证人可靠性、Gendry外貌或Eddard血缘推断。

blocks.original_text: “The bald one? No, not him. He never said no word, just glared at me, like I was some raper who done for his daughter.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Gendry称bald one沉默怒视，像把自己当伤害其女儿的raper。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Stannis不问只glare，可能因为已形成判断，也可能只是性格；文本未给动机。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在tourney治理、Jon与Stannis调查轨迹、证人可靠性、Gendry外貌或Eddard血缘推断。

blocks.original_text: “Mind your filthy tongue,” the master said. “This is the King’s own Hand.” The boy lowered his eyes. “A smart boy, but stubborn. That helm . . . the others call him bullheaded, so he threw it in their teeth.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Mott训斥语言并解释boy虽聪明固执，bull helm是回应bullheaded外号。
blocks.literary_note_zh: forge中Gendry以黑发蓝眼、力量、bull helm和固执登场；Jon曾专问其mother，Eddard由外貌形成血缘判断。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在tourney治理、Jon与Stannis调查轨迹、证人可靠性、Gendry外貌或Eddard血缘推断。

blocks.original_text: Ned touched the boy’s head, fingering the thick black hair. “Look at me, Gendry.” The apprentice lifted his face. Ned studied the shape of his jaw, the eyes like blue ice. Yes, he thought, I see it. “Go back to your work, lad. I’m sorry to have bothered you.” He walked back to the house with the master. “Who paid the boy’s apprentice fee?” he asked lightly.
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard摸其厚黑发、看蓝眼和jaw后心想已看出关系，随后轻问谁付apprentice fee。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard的Yes, I see it表明他主观认出Robert特征；这是强烈推断，尚无公开承认或正式证明。
blocks.background_zh: **事实／推断：** Eddard从Gendry外貌认出Robert特征并推断其为king’s bastard；尚无Robert确认或出生记录。

blocks.original_text: Mott looked fretful. “You saw the boy. Such a strong boy. Those hands of his, those hands were made for hammers. He had such promise, I took him on without a fee.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Mott先声称因Gendry强壮有天赋而免fee。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Mott承认匿名lord付双倍gold买收徒与silence，却拒绝定义Gendry过去；Eddard保护其现有身份并确认找到king’s bastard线索。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在tourney治理、Jon与Stannis调查轨迹、证人可靠性、Gendry外貌或Eddard血缘推断。

blocks.original_text: “The truth now,” Ned urged. “The streets are full of strong boys. The day you take on an apprentice without a fee will be the day the Wall comes down. Who paid for him?”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard指出街上强壮少年很多、Mott不可能免费，逼其说truth。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Mott承认匿名lord付双倍gold买收徒与silence，却拒绝定义Gendry过去；Eddard保护其现有身份并确认找到king’s bastard线索。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在tourney治理、Jon与Stannis调查轨迹、证人可靠性、Gendry外貌或Eddard血缘推断。

blocks.original_text: “A lord,” the master said reluctantly. “He gave no name, and wore no sigil on his coat. He paid in gold, twice the customary sum, and said he was paying once for the boy, and once for my silence.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Mott承认匿名lord付双倍gold，一份收徒、一份买silence。
blocks.literary_note_zh: 双倍fee加silence表明有人有意保障Gendry生活并隐藏资助来源。
blocks.background_zh: **资助：** 匿名lord付double apprentice fee并另付silence money，表明Gendry入坊被刻意安排。

blocks.original_text: “Describe him.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard要求描述。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Mott承认匿名lord付双倍gold买收徒与silence，却拒绝定义Gendry过去；Eddard保护其现有身份并确认找到king’s bastard线索。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在tourney治理、Jon与Stannis调查轨迹、证人可靠性、Gendry外貌或Eddard血缘推断。

blocks.original_text: “He was stout, round of shoulder, not so tall as you. Brown beard, but there was a bit of red in it, I’ll swear. He wore a rich cloak, that I do remember, heavy purple velvet worked with silver threads, but the hood shadowed his face and I never did see him clear.” He hesitated a moment. “My lord, I want no trouble.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Mott描述矮壮圆肩、棕红beard、紫银rich cloak但脸藏hood的人，并说不想惹麻烦。
blocks.literary_note_zh: anonymous lord描述可用于未来识别，但hood使Mott从未清楚见脸，证词精度有限。
blocks.background_zh: **描述限制：** sponsor被描述为矮壮、棕红胡须、紫银cloak，但hood遮脸，Mott不能确认身份。

blocks.original_text: “None of us wants trouble, but I fear these are troubled times, Master Mott,” Ned said. “You know who the boy is.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard说时代已麻烦，并断言Mott知道boy是谁。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Mott承认匿名lord付双倍gold买收徒与silence，却拒绝定义Gendry过去；Eddard保护其现有身份并确认找到king’s bastard线索。

blocks.original_text: “I am only an armorer, my lord. I know what I’m told.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Mott自称只是armorer，只知别人告诉的事。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Mott承认匿名lord付双倍gold买收徒与silence，却拒绝定义Gendry过去；Eddard保护其现有身份并确认找到king’s bastard线索。

blocks.original_text: “You know who the boy is,” Ned repeated patiently. “That is not a question.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard重复这不是question。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Mott承认匿名lord付双倍gold买收徒与silence，却拒绝定义Gendry过去；Eddard保护其现有身份并确认找到king’s bastard线索。

blocks.original_text: “The boy is my apprentice,” the master said. He looked Ned in the eye, stubborn as old iron. “Who he was before he came to me, that’s none of my concern.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Mott坚持Gendry只是apprentice，过去身份与自己无关。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Mott用present craft identity保护Gendry：apprentice比possible royal bastard更安全、更可控。
blocks.background_zh: 本段没有新增必须补充的世界观事实；重点在tourney治理、Jon与Stannis调查轨迹、证人可靠性、Gendry外貌或Eddard血缘推断。

blocks.original_text: Ned nodded. He decided that he liked Tobho Mott, master armorer. “If the day ever comes when Gendry would rather wield a sword than forge one, send him to me. He has the look of a warrior. Until then, you have my thanks, Master Mott, and my promise. Should I ever want a helm to frighten children, this will be the first place I visit.”
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard因其保护boy而喜欢Mott，并承诺若Gendry想持剑可送来。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Eddard尊重其沉默，说明他理解保护boy优先于强迫公开身世；sword offer也根据visible strength延伸。

blocks.original_text: His guard was waiting outside with the horses. “Did you find anything, my lord?” Jacks asked as Ned mounted up.
blocks.explanation_zh: guards问是否找到什么。
blocks.literary_note_zh: Mott承认匿名lord付双倍gold买收徒与silence，却拒绝定义Gendry过去；Eddard保护其现有身份并确认找到king’s bastard线索。

blocks.original_text: “I did,” Ned told him, wondering. What had Jon Arryn wanted with a king’s bastard, and why was it worth his life?
blocks.explanation_zh: Eddard确认找到，并思考Jon为何调查king’s bastard、此事为何值得死亡。
blocks.literary_note_zh: king’s bastard是Eddard内心调查结论，不是Gendry本人知情、Robert承认或法律裁定。
